behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the file path (packages/solid-router/src/headContentUtils.tsx), the function (useTags), the import (replaceEqualDeep from @tanstack/router-core), Solid.createMemo with prev parameter, and replaceEqualDeep(prev, next) — all specifics asserted in the tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md's 'Test Requirements' section explicitly states the exact fix: import replaceEqualDeep, return Solid.createMemo with prev parameter, call replaceEqualDeep(prev, next). This is not describing what's broken — it's prescribing the exact implementation changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact syntax patterns like 'return Solid.createMemo((prev', 'replaceEqualDeep(prev, next)' via regex. An alternative correct fix using different variable names (e.g., 'previous' instead of 'prev', or 'current' instead of 'next'), or using a different memoization approach, would fail the regex-based tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The three fail-to-pass tests (test_use_tags_imports_replace_equal_deep, test_use_tags_returns_create_memo, test_use_tags_calls_replace_equal_deep) are purely text-matching via regex on file contents with no code execution. They use patterns like `assert re.search(r"...", content)` and `assert "replaceEqualDeep" in content`.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check file text content via regex. An agent could write arbitrary non-functional code containing the matched strings (e.g., a comment '// replaceEqualDeep(prev, next)') and pass all three f2p tests without actually fixing the bug. The pass-to-pass tests do run the build/test suite but aren't f2p.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three code pattern requirements listed in instruction.md are tested (import, createMemo with prev, replaceEqualDeep call), plus build/test/lint/type checks are verified.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with comments into 'FAIL-TO-PASS TESTS' and 'PASS-TO-PASS TESTS' sections, with descriptive function names and docstrings.
